text: The United States and Israel launched a new wave of strikes on Iran Saturday night, escalating a conflict that has already killed Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei.
World leaders are now warning that the strikes risk triggering a wider regional conflict, with European allies stressing they did not join the operation.
Afghanistan's Taliban government said the war will destabilize the entire region and called for an immediate ceasefire.
Oil prices surged past one hundred and twenty dollars a barrel as markets fear the biggest energy shock in decades.
Analysts say the coming days will determine whether this becomes a contained operation or spirals into a full-scale Middle Eastern war.
The killing of Khamenei has left Iran without its top decision-maker at a critical moment.
With no clear successor and retaliatory strikes ongoing, the power vacuum could either push Iran toward negotiations or toward an even more aggressive response.
